narrative.weakest_link: Beat 5 is the most fragile part of the chain: it is structurally usable, but only as a method-and-pilot bridge. The cited spine supports a provenance-sensitive, fixed-compute experiment design more than it supports a strong causal claim about provenance itself, because provenance remains confounded with curation quality, alignment recipe, and benchmark sensitivity.
narrative.suggested_fix: Keep Beat 5 explicitly diagnostic: say it motivates a controlled pilot rather than proving the main thesis. In the outline, add one limiting transition from Beat 4 to Beat 5 that states the literature does not yet provide clean provenance-only disentanglement, and one forward transition from Beat 5 to Beat 6 that separates pilot evidence from deployment/infrastructure claims.

contradictions_found.issue: Filtered and deduplicated web-only pretraining can still work well, which undercuts any blanket claim that web contamination already makes web pretraining unreliable.
contradictions_found.evidence: Limiter 5 cites filtered/documented web corpora as a direct complication and tells the writer to narrow the claim to unfiltered or search-mediated exposure.

contradictions_found.issue: Model collapse is conditional: curated or self-correcting synthetic loops can mitigate or avoid collapse, so recursive-use results do not generalize to all synthetic data practice.
contradictions_found.evidence: Limiter 2 and Beat 1 both distinguish indiscriminate reuse from curated/self-correcting regimes and quantify both collapse-risk and mitigation support.

contradictions_found.issue: Open-web contamination is not yet cleanly measurable at scale; detector success in controlled settings does not imply robust web-scale detection under adversarial conditions.
contradictions_found.evidence: Limiter 1 clearly separates cooperative/provider-controlled detection from adversarial open-web conditions and warns against overstating current tooling.

contradictions_found.issue: Evidence for live-web drift/degradation is still partial; the corpus lacks a direct post-2022 web-scale contamination audit.
contradictions_found.evidence: Limiter 3 and Beat 2 explicitly narrow the claim to rising risk plus incomplete measurement rather than demonstrated general training-source failure.

closest_existing_work: LIMA: Less Is More for Alignment
OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Models in the Open
Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
RLAIF vs. RLHF: Scaling Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with AI Feedback
Position: Data Authenticity, Consent, & Provenance for AI are all broken: what will it take to fix them?
Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute Optimally can be More Effective than

补搜_queries: Category I / Beat 6: human-data value beyond simulated or AI feedback; subgroup-faithful preference collection; contested norms; disagreement-preserving alignment datasets
Category I / Beat 6: empirical comparisons of human vs AI feedback on social reasoning, moral judgment, or culturally diverse preference tasks

Beat 3 / framework validation: L_auth or data-authenticity effects in fine-tuning; causal studies isolating provenance/authenticity from size and quality; cross-stage validation linking pretraining and post-training authenticity
Beat 2: post-2022 longitudinal audits of live-web AI-content drift and contamination rates at web scale; temporal measurement of training-source degradation
Beat 2: robust contamination measurement under adversarial open-web conditions; provenance or retrieval-based detection versus brittle classifier-based detection
Beat 7 / Category K: provenance-aware data collection, verified-human pipelines, or intervention studies showing end-to-end gains from authentic human data in socially grounded post-training

overselling_detected: Beat 1 would be overstated if written as 'synthetic data is harmful' rather than 'indiscriminate or weakly controlled recursive reuse creates collapse risk'; the corpus also includes mitigation/curation results.

Beat 2 would be overstated if written as demonstrated web-wide degradation. The corpus supports rising contamination risk and partial measurability in bounded domains, not a direct post-2022 web-scale audit showing general training failure.

Beat 3 would be overstated if L_auth were presented as a validated law or stage-agnostic causal model. The corpus supports it as a scoped, descriptive framework for organizing authenticity/composition effects, especially around fine-tuning.

Beats 4-5 would be overstated if they implied human data is always necessary or that AI-feedback methods fail generally. The corpus supports a narrower claim: real human, provenance-rich data appears especially valuable for socially grounded, contested, subgroup-sensitive tasks; pilot evidence is directional, not dispositive.

Beat 6 would be overstated if deployment of CampusGo were converted into evidence of downstream model gains. Beat 7 would be understated if inference-time scaling, CoT, self-consistency, search, supervision, and steering were not treated as real competing explanations.
honesty.what_author_should_admit: The corpus is strong enough for a cautious paper but not for maximal causal claims. It supports motivation from recursive-contamination risk, partial evidence of web drift, a descriptive authenticity framework, and a narrow primary thesis that provenance-rich human data is especially useful on socially grounded post-training tasks. It does not yet validate L_auth as a law, prove broad web degradation, show that human data universally dominates curated synthetic/AI-feedback data, or attribute any observed gains uniquely to provenance rather than inference-time reasoning or other alignment mechanisms.
